questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank: The old house was dilapidated, but the restoration team worked hard to make it ________.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: "Dilapidated" means in poor condition or ruined due to neglect. Its antonym is "pristine," which means clean, fresh, or in perfect condition. Other options like "shabby," "crumbling," and "worn" are similar in meaning to "dilapidated," not its opposite.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that correctly expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The judges will declare her the winner of the competition.
options: The winner of the competition will be declared her by the judges.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She will declared the winner of the competition by the judges.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She will be declared the winner of the competition by the judges.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The competition’s winner will be declared her by the judges.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is: "She will be declared the winner of the competition by the judges." Explanation: In active voice, the subject (the judges) performs the action. When converted to passive voice: • The object in the active sentence (her) becomes the subject in the passive sentence. • The structure for passive voice in future tense is: [subject] + will be + [past participle] + [by + agent].

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Obsolete
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: The word "obsolete" refers to something that is no longer in use or has become outdated due to newer alternatives or advancements. Among the options, "outdated" is the synonym that most closely matches this meaning.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank. The detective's hypothesis seemed plausible, but after further investigation, it was proven ________.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: "Plausible" means something that is believable or appears reasonable. The antonym is "absurd," which refers to something completely unreasonable or illogical. The other options—"logical," "reasonable," and "believable"—are actually synonyms or closely related to the meaning of "plausible," not its opposite.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the phrase in bold. After lying about his qualifications, John realized he was about to face the music.
options: To listen to and appreciate a musical performance
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To participate in a celebration or party
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To enjoy the spotlight or attention
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To deal with the consequences of one's actions
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d Explanation: The phrase "face the music" is an idiom that means accepting responsibility or consequences for one's actions, often unpleasant ones. In the context of the sentence, John lied about his qualifications, and now he must confront the repercussions of his dishonesty.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the following sentence in active voice. The reports are reviewed by the team daily.
options: The team will review the reports daily.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The team reviews the reports daily.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The reports are reviewing by the team daily.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The team is reviewed by the reports daily.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: In the given sentence, the subject (the reports) is receiving the action (reviewed) in the passive voice. To convert it into active voice: • The structure should be: Subject (the team) + verb (reviews) + object (the reports) + time marker (daily). • "The team" becomes the subject performing the action, and "the reports" remains the object. Correct Answer: The team reviews the reports daily.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one word substitute for the given group of words. A speech given by a person at a funeral to honour the deceased.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: A "eulogy" is a speech or piece of writing that praises someone who has recently died. Other options have distinct meanings: • "Epitaph" refers to an inscription on a gravestone. • "Elegy" is a mournful poem. • "Obituary" is a published notice of death. Correct Answer: Eulogy

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the appropriate article from the below options to complete the sentence. Mark “No article” if there is no requirement of article in the given sentence. ___ only tea I like is black tea.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: The definite article "the" is used to refer to something specific or particular. In the given sentence, "the only tea I like" indicates a specific type of tea (black tea) that the speaker prefers. Hence, "the" is the most appropriate article to complete the sentence.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. The box containing / the apples from / the valleys of Kashmir were / thrown away by Gautam.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} the valleys of Kashmir were
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: The error lies in subject-verb agreement. The subject of the sentence is "The box", which is singular. Hence, the verb should also be singular (was) instead of were. The corrected sentence should read: "The box containing the apples from the valleys of Kashmir was thrown away by Gautam."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. 1.Globalisation refers to the interconnectedness of nations, economies and cultures around the world 2.However, globalisation has also created challenges, such as job displacement, economic inequality and cultural homogenisation. 3.It has brought about many benefits, such as increased trade and economic growth, greater access to goods and services, and cultural exchange. 4.It is important to address these issues and promote responsible globalisation that benefits everyone.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d Explanation: • Sentence 1 introduces the topic of globalisation, defining it as the interconnectedness of nations, economies, and cultures. This is the logical starting point for the paragraph. • Sentence 3 follows by explaining the benefits of globalisation, such as increased trade, economic growth, and cultural exchange. This builds on the definition provided in Sentence 1. • Sentence 2 introduces the challenges or downsides of globalisation, such as job displacement, economic inequality, and cultural homogenisation. This provides a balanced view after discussing the benefits. • Sentence 4 concludes the paragraph by suggesting the need to address these challenges and promote responsible globalisation. This is the logical conclusion after discussing both the benefits and challenges. Thus, the correct order is 1-3-2-4.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is: Acquiesce. Explanation: "Acquiesce" means to agree or comply silently or without protest. It is the correctly spelled word among the given options.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} What does the idiom "Kick the bucket" mean?
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: The idiom "kick the bucket" is an informal expression that means to die. Its origin is debated, but one theory links it to the use of a bucket in old execution methods or to the motion of a bucket being kicked accidentally during a struggle. Regardless of its origin, the phrase is widely recognized as a euphemism for death.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following questions, a sentence has been given in direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. She said, "I will have finished my homework by the time you arrive."
options: She said that she would have finished her homework by the time I arrived.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She said that she will have finished her homework by the time I arrive.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She said that she would finish her homework by the time I arrived.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She said that she will finish her homework by the time I arrive.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: When converting a sentence from direct to indirect speech: • The reporting verb "said" in the past tense indicates a shift in tenses within the reported clause. • The future perfect tense "will have finished" changes to "would have finished" in indirect speech, as it follows the rule of backshifting tenses. • The pronoun "you" changes to "I", based on the context of the speaker and listener. Correct Answer: She said that she would have finished her homework by the time I arrived.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. NMC has also included the provision of the formation of student associations in all the medical colleges to support students with their general welfare. B. The National Medical Commission (NMC) has issued draft regulations for the MBBS curriculum. C. According to the regulations, students would only have four attempts to pass the first year and they will not be allowed to continue the course beyond nine years. D. Migration to a medical college in a different state or district will be allowed only to meritorious students.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d Explanation: • Sentence B introduces the topic by stating that the National Medical Commission (NMC) has issued draft regulations for the MBBS curriculum. This is the logical starting point for the paragraph. • Sentence C follows by providing specific details about the regulations, such as the number of attempts allowed to pass the first year and the maximum duration of the course. This builds on the introduction provided in Sentence B. • Sentence A adds another provision of the regulations, mentioning the formation of student associations to support students' welfare. This is a natural continuation after discussing the academic rules. • Sentence D concludes the paragraph by explaining the migration policy for meritorious students. This is the logical ending after discussing other aspects of the regulations. Thus, the correct order is B-C-A-D.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Alacrity
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: "Alacrity" means cheerful readiness, promptness, or willingness to do something. It conveys enthusiasm and eagerness in approaching a task or activity. Other options explained: • Hesitation: It implies doubt or delay in taking action, which is opposite in meaning to "alacrity." • Reluctance: This indicates unwillingness or resistance, which is also the opposite of "alacrity." • Delay: Refers to postponement or slowing down, which contrasts with the readiness implied by "alacrity."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. While they're incredibly unusual, moonbows (or lunar rainbows) are caused by the reflection, refraction and dispersion of light and tend to happen most often in places with waterfalls and mist. B. There also needs to be a near full moon for there to be enough light for you to see it. C. If a storm is passing and the sun starts shining, you might be lucky enough to spot a rainbow. D. But did you know that you can see something just as amazing at night?
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: • Sentence C introduces the topic by mentioning the possibility of spotting a rainbow during the day when a storm passes and the sun shines. This is the logical starting point for the paragraph. • Sentence D follows by posing a question about something equally amazing that can be seen at night, creating a smooth transition from day to night phenomena. • Sentence A explains what this nighttime phenomenon is (moonbows) and how they are formed, building on the curiosity raised in Sentence D. • Sentence B adds the condition required to see a moonbow, which is a near full moon, concluding the explanation. Thus, the correct order is C-D-A-B.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The teacher asked the students to complete their work quick.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} to complete their work quick
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} to completing their work quickly
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: • The word "quick" is an adjective, which is used to describe nouns (e.g., a quick response). However, in this sentence, it is incorrectly used to describe the verb "complete". • The correct form to describe a verb is an adverb, which is "quickly". Adverbs modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs, and they often end in -ly. • Therefore, the correct phrase should be "to complete their work quickly".

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Identify the sentence with the correct subject-verb agreement.
options: The group of students have gone on a trip.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The group of students are going on a trip.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The group of students were going on a trip.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The group of students is going on a trip.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d Explanation: In this sentence, the subject is "The group," which is a collective noun. Collective nouns are usually treated as singular and take a singular verb, especially when the group is being considered as a single entity. Therefore, the correct verb here is "is." Correct Answer: The group of students is going on a trip.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correct spelling to fill in the blank. The teacher warned that such behaviour might result in serious ________.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: The correct spelling is "consequences," which means the outcomes or effects that follow an action or decision. In the given context, it refers to the serious outcomes of the behaviour mentioned.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following questions, a sentence has been given in direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. Rahul said, "Where did you find this book? I have been searching for it for weeks."
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } Rahul asked where I had found that book and said that he had been searching for it for weeks.

*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul said where I found that book and told that he had been searching for it for weeks.

*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul asked where he found that book and said that he had been searching for it for weeks.

*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rahul asked where he had found that book and told that he had been searching for it for weeks.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math';src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf ') format('truetype'); } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: "Rahul asked where he had found that book and said that he had been searching for it for weeks." Explanation: • Since Rahul is asking a question, "said" changes to "asked." • The tense of "did you find" becomes "I had found" (past perfect). • "This book" changes to "that book" for indirect speech. • The second statement remains in past perfect continuous tense.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that (1) __________ many people for centuries. The stories, which (2) __________ passed down through generations, are full of adventure, drama, and lessons about life. One of the most famous myths is about Hercules, who (3) __________ twelve impossible tasks to prove his strength. Another well-known story involves Athena, the goddess of wisdom, who (4) __________ from Zeus's head fully grown. The myth of Persephone, who (5) __________ to spend part of the year in the underworld, explains the changing seasons. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 1 is "has fascinated." Explanation: • The phrase "Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that" requires a verb in the present perfect tense because it refers to an action that started in the past and continues up to the present. • "Has fascinated" (present perfect) expresses this ongoing effect over time, meaning Greek mythology has captured the interest of many people for centuries up to now.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that (1) __________ many people for centuries. The stories, which (2) __________ passed down through generations, are full of adventure, drama, and lessons about life. One of the most famous myths is about Hercules, who (3) __________ twelve impossible tasks to prove his strength. Another well-known story involves Athena, the goddess of wisdom, who (4) __________ from Zeus's head fully grown. The myth of Persephone, who (5) __________ spend part of the year in the underworld, explains the changing seasons. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 2 is "were." Explanation: The phrase "which __________ passed down through generations" refers to an action in the past that was repeatedly done over time. Since we are talking about a past action, "were" (past tense) is the correct choice. This indicates that the stories were passed down over many generations.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that (1) __________ many people for centuries. The stories, which (2) __________ passed down through generations, are full of adventure, drama, and lessons about life. One of the most famous myths is about Hercules, who (3) __________ twelve impossible tasks to prove his strength. Another well-known story involves Athena, the goddess of wisdom, who (4) __________ from Zeus's head fully grown. The myth of Persephone, who (5) __________ spend part of the year in the underworld, explains the changing seasons. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 3 is "completed." Explanation: The sentence is referring to a past action, specifically a mythological event that took place. Since the action is completed in the past (Hercules performed the tasks), we need to use the past tense verb "completed" to indicate that the action is finished.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that (1) __________ many people for centuries. The stories, which (2) __________ passed down through generations, are full of adventure, drama, and lessons about life. One of the most famous myths is about Hercules, who (3) __________ twelve impossible tasks to prove his strength. Another well-known story involves Athena, the goddess of wisdom, who (4) __________ from Zeus's head fully grown. The myth of Persephone, who (5) __________ spend part of the year in the underworld, explains the changing seasons. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 4 is "was born." Explanation: The sentence is describing an event in the past when Athena came into existence, specifically when she was born from Zeus's head. Since this is a past event, we need to use the past tense "was born."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Greek mythology is a fascinating subject that (1) __________ many people for centuries. The stories, which (2) __________ passed down through generations, are full of adventure, drama, and lessons about life. One of the most famous myths is about Hercules, who (3) __________ twelve impossible tasks to prove his strength. Another well-known story involves Athena, the goddess of wisdom, who (4) __________ from Zeus's head fully grown. The myth of Persephone, who (5) __________ spend part of the year in the underworld, explains the changing seasons. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 5 is "had to." Explanation: The sentence describes a past necessity or obligation in the myth of Persephone. The use of "had to" indicates that it was a requirement for her to spend part of the year in the underworld. This refers to a past event or condition, which makes "had to" the appropriate choice.
